articles.title: Russia Probes Two Car Bombs In Moscow
articles.content:
莫斯科6月10日（路透社）——俄罗斯调查人员周三表示，他们已逮捕了莫斯科汽车炸弹爆炸案至少两名嫌疑人，拘留了被国内安全部门称被诱骗安放炸弹的青少年。

周二莫斯科发生了两起汽车炸弹爆炸事件——一起发生在早上，地点在莫斯科东部；另一起被安全部门在莫斯科西南部发现。

俄罗斯国家调查委员会表示，已就莫斯科西南部针对一家科研生产企业员工的炸弹爆炸案提起刑事诉讼。目前尚不清楚该装置如何引爆。

调查确定，一名少女被身份不明的人要求取走炸弹，并将其交给一名少年，该少年将炸弹连同GPS追踪器一起放在车上。爆炸未造成人员伤亡，嫌疑人已被起诉。

据《共青团真理报》报道，东部莫斯科另一起汽车炸弹事件中有一名司机遇难，但受害者姓名尚未公布。

克里姆林宫发言人德米特里·佩斯科夫表示确实发生了爆炸，但调查期间无法透露细节。他说：“确实发生了爆炸，但正如你所理解的，鉴于正在进行的调查，相关细节不便披露。这当然是特种部门的事。”

目前尚不清楚第二起炸弹案是否有人被捕。

自2022年战争开始以来，乌克兰军事情报部门声称对暗杀多名俄罗斯高级军官负责，其中一些军官曾出现在乌克兰公布的俄方目标名单上。

（路透社报道；编辑：盖伊·福尔科恩伯里奇、艾丹·刘易斯）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

MOSCOW, June 10 (Reuters) - Russian investigators said on Wednesday that they had arrested at least two suspects behind a car bombing in Moscow, detaining teenagers who the domestic security service said were convinced to plant the bomb.

There were two car bombs on Tuesday in Moscow - one which detonated in the morning in eastern Moscow and one which was identified by security services in southwestern Moscow.

Advertisement

Russia’s state Investigative Committee said that a criminal case had been opened in relation to the bomb in southwestern Moscow which targeted an employee of a scientific production enterprise. It was not clear how that device detonated.

The investigation established that a teenage girl was told by unidentified people to pick up the bomb and handed it to a teenage boy who placed it on the car along with a GPS tracker, the committee said.

There were no casualties and the suspects have been charged.

Advertisement

In a separate car bomb in eastern Moscow, a driver was killed, according to the Kommersant newspaper, though the victim has not been named.

Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said that there had been an explosion but that the details could not be disclosed while an investigation was ongoing.

“An explosion took place, but the details, as you understand, are not subject to disclosure in connection with the investigation that is underway,” Peskov said. “Of course, this is a matter for our special services.”

Advertisement

It was unclear whether any arrests had been made in connection to the second bomb.

Since the start of the war in 2022, Ukrainian military intelligence has claimed responsibility for assassinating several senior Russian officers, some of whom have appeared on a public list of Ukraine’s enemies.

(Reporting by Reuters; editing by Guy Faulconbridge, Aidan Lewis)

Related

Russia

Moscow

car bomb

A Year After Trump’s Scolding, It Turns Out Zelenskyy Has All Kinds Of Cards
Ukrainian Attacks Spark Fires At Oil Facilities In Russia, Crimea

articles.title: Stabbing Suspect Appears In Court After Night Of Anti-Immigrant Protests In Northern Ireland
articles.content:
贝尔法斯特消息——一名来自苏丹的30岁男子周三在贝尔法斯特一家法院出庭，他被控谋杀未遂罪名。这起持刀袭击事件导致一名受害者身受重伤，并在北爱尔兰多地引发了反移民暴力事件。

30岁的哈迪·阿洛迪德在贝尔法斯特治安法院通过视频出庭后被下令还押候审。检察官表示，他被指控在持刀袭击中刺瞎斯蒂芬·奥格尔维的左眼。此外，他还被指控在同一天威胁杀害一名放射技师，以及非法持有刀具。

阿洛迪德拒绝通过阿拉伯语翻译获得法律代理，也没有进行答辩。

此次出庭前一夜发生暴力事件。蒙面男子纵火焚烧了多所他们认为住有移民的房屋，焚烧垃圾桶，点燃一辆贝尔法斯特公交车，并向警察投掷物品。消防人员从燃烧的房屋中救出了数人。

来自刚果民主共和国的贝尔法斯特居民安塞尔姆·希马表示，他在自家附近看到焚烧车辆冒出的浓烟。他说：“我在这条街上住了快十年了，和邻居们关系很好，但昨晚太可怕了。我们不知道该怎么办，我很害怕。看到这一切，我在想自己会不会是下一个。”

北爱尔兰权力共享政府双方政界人士都谴责了这些暴力事件。新芬党爱尔兰民族主义者第一部长米歇尔·奥尼尔称之为“流氓行径”。她说：“一群蒙面男子将家庭从家中烧出去，这无异于令人厌恶的懦夫行为。”

亲英的民主统一党副第一部长艾玛·利特尔-彭格利表示，“将一个人邪恶行为的愤怒发泄在那些毫无关系的人身上是完全错误的”。

袭击事件被视频记录

周一的袭击被视频画面记录下来，并在社交媒体上迅速传播。40多岁的奥格尔维面部和背部被刺伤后住院治疗。警方表示，阿洛迪德于2023年从邻国爱尔兰共和国进入北爱尔兰，申请了庇护并获得了5年居留许可。

北爱尔兰警察局表示，没有信息表明这起袭击与恐怖主义有关，警方也没有在寻找其他嫌疑人。

尽管政界人士呼吁保持冷静，街头暴力还是爆发了。

首相斯塔默谴责这起持刀袭击“令人作呕”，但他表示不会容忍基于人们背景的暴力行为。斯塔默在X平台上写道：“昨晚贝尔法斯特的场景令人震惊，完全不可接受。没有任何理由可以为威胁我们社区的暴力和混乱辩护，那些在网络或其他地方煽动暴力的人同样不可原谅。很明显，昨晚人们因为背景而成为目标，我不会容忍这种行为。”

包括斯蒂芬·亚克斯利-列侬（又名汤米·罗宾逊）在内的极右翼活动人士在网上煽动抗议活动。

北爱尔兰司法部长娜奥米·隆表示，那些“昨天可能连贝尔法斯特在地图上都找不到”的社交媒体煽动者正在“利用”当地人的恐惧。她对BBC表示：“如果你仅仅因为人们的肤色就把他们从家中赶走，你无法用任何其他方式粉饰，那就是种族主义，那些别有用心的人需要退后一步。”

关于爱尔兰边境问题

一些政界人士表示，这起持刀袭击事件应促使人们重新审视北爱尔兰（属于英国一部分）与爱尔兰共和国之间的开放边境。

边境是一个非常敏感的问题。允许人员自由流动是结束数十年暴力冲突的和平进程的主要支柱。这场涉及爱尔兰共和派、英国保皇派武装分子和英国安全部队的冲突在1998年和平协议之前造成近3600人死亡。周二的大部分暴力事件发生在工人阶层社区，那里的前准军事组织仍然对街头有很大影响力。

上周，英格兰南安普顿一名大学生12月在另一起案件中被刺死的事件也被活动人士和美国副总统万斯利用，他将这些暴力事件归咎于移民。

亨利·诺瓦克是白人，他被维克拉姆·迪格瓦杀害。迪格瓦是锡克教徒，他向警方虚假声称自己是诺瓦克种族主义袭击的受害者。当警察到达时，他们最初将受伤的诺瓦克视为嫌疑人，直到注意到他的伤势后才试图进行抢救。

迪格瓦因用锡克教匕首刺杀诺瓦克被判谋杀罪成立，上周被判处终身监禁，最低刑期21年。但此案引发了关于警务和种族问题的激烈争论，一场关于诺瓦克之死的抗议活动演变为暴力事件，一些人用椅子和石头袭击警察。数人因暴力扰乱秩序被起诉。
articles.summary:
BELFAST, Northern Ireland (AP) — A 30-year-old man from Sudan appeared in a Belfast court Wednesday charged with attempted murder over a stabbing attack that left a victim seriously injured and triggered anti-immigrant violence in several parts of Northern Ireland.

Hadi Alodid, 30, was ordered held in jail after an appearance by video in Belfast Magistrates’ Court, where he was accused of blinding Stephen Ogilvie in his left eye during the stabbing, prosecutors said. He was also charged with threatening to kill a radiographer on the same day and with possessing a knife.

Advertisement

Alodid refused legal representation through an Arabic interpreter and did not enter a plea.

The court appearance followed a night of violence in which masked men set fire to several homes they believed to house immigrants, burned trash bins, torched a Belfast bus and pelted police with objects. Firefighters rescued several people from burning homes.

Anselme Shima, a Belfast resident originally from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, said he saw smoke from burning vehicles near his home.

Advertisement

“I’ve lived on my street for almost 10 years, I have a good relationship with my neighbors, but last night was a horrific one,” he said. “We don’t know what to do. I’m scared. Seeing this, I’m wondering if I’m next.”

Politicians from both parts of Northern Ireland’s power-sharing government condemned the violence. First Minister Michelle O’Neill of Irish nationalist party Sinn Fein said it was “thuggery.”

“Groups of masked men burning families out of their homes is nothing less than disgusting cowardice,” she said.

Advertisement

Deputy First Minister Emma Little-Pengelly, of the pro-British Democratic Unionist Party, said that “taking frustration at the evil actions of a person out on those who had no part in it is utterly wrong.”

The Attack Was Caught On Video

Monday’s attack, caught in graphic video footage that quickly spread on social media, was seized on by anti-immigration activists. Ogilvie, a man in his 40s, was hospitalized with serious injuries to his face and back after being stabbed.

Advertisement

Police said Alodid entered Northern Ireland from the neighboring Republic of Ireland in 2023, applied for asylum and was given a 5-year permit to remain.

The Police Service of Northern Ireland said there is no information to suggest the attack is terrorism-related and they are not seeking other suspects.

The street violence erupted despite calls from politicians for calm.

Prime Minister Starmer condemned the stabbing attack as “sickening,” but said violence against people based on their background would not be tolerated.

Advertisement

“The scenes in Belfast last night were shocking and completely unacceptable,” Starmer said on X. “There is no justification for the violence and disorder that we saw threatening our communities, nor for those who encouraged it, online or elsewhere. It is clear that people were targeted last night because of their background and I will not tolerate it.”

Protests were encouraged online by far-right activists including Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, also known as Tommy Robinson.

Northern Ireland Justice Minister Naomi Long said social media agitators who “yesterday would have struggled to find Belfast on a map” were “weaponizing” the fears of local people.

Advertisement

“If you’re driving people from their homes based on nothing but the color of their skin, you can’t dress that up any other way, it’s racism, and those bad faith actors need to take a step back,” she told the BBC.

Questions About The Irish Border

Some politicians said the stabbing should spark a review of the open border between Northern Ireland, which is part of the U.K., and the Republic of Ireland.

Advertisement

The border is a highly sensitive issue. Allowing the free flow of people back and forth is a major pillar of the peace process that largely ended decades of violence known as “The Troubles.” The conflict involving Irish Republican and British Loyalist militants and U.K. security forces left almost 3,600 people dead before a 1998 peace accord.

Much of Tuesday’s violence took place in working-class areas where former paramilitary groups still hold considerable sway over the streets.

Last week a separate case of a university student who was stabbed to death in Southampton, England in December was seized on by activists and U.S. Vice President JD Vance,who blamed immigration for the violence.

Advertisement

Henry Nowak, who was white, was killed by Vickrum Digwa, a Sikh who falsely claimed to police that he was the victim of a racist assault by Nowak. When police officers arrived, they initially treated the wounded Nowak as a suspect before noticing his injury and trying to resuscitate him.

Digwa was convicted of murder for stabbing Nowak with a Sikh dagger and sentenced last week to life in prison with a minimum 21-year term. But the case has spurred heated debates about policing and race, and a protest over Nowak’s death turned violent with some attacking police with chairs and rocks. Several people were charged with violent disorder over the protest.

___

Lawless reported from London.

Advertisement

Related

Northern Ireland

British Deputy Prime Minister Tells JD Vance He Was Wrong To Blame Immigration For Teen's Murder
Hegseth Slammed For Slipping Anti-Migration Message Into D-Day Tribute

Adblock test (Why?)

articles.title: U.S. Launches New Strikes On Iran, Which Fires Back At Gulf States
articles.content:
迪拜，阿拉伯联合酋长国（美联社）——美国在周四凌晨对伊朗发动了第二轮空袭。此前总统唐纳德·特朗普警告称德黑兰将为谈判停滞“付出代价”，伊朗则以袭击巴林和科威特作为回应。

新一轮美国对多个伊朗城市的攻击发生时，谈判结束战争的努再次陷入僵局。伊朗坚持维持对霍尔木兹海峡的控制，这已扰乱全球能源供应并推高了油价。

这是本周内来回袭击第三次考验为期两个月的停火协议。

特朗普敦促伊朗签署结束战争的协议，并暗示本周早些时候可能达成协议。伊朗驻联合国大使表示，美国如果想要达成协议，就应该停止威胁使用武力。

大使阿米尔·赛义德·伊拉瓦尼周三在联合国安理会表示：“伊朗从未在威胁和压力下进行谈判，也永远不会屈服于压力或质询。”

尽管如此，两国似乎都在寻找结束冲突的方法——如果他们能将其包装为国内胜利的话。

伊朗尽管遭受数周猛烈轰炸，仍表现出韧性。该国押注其封锁霍尔木兹海峡的能力——这是石油和天然气的关键通道——将为其提供强有力的谈判筹码。

以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡似乎志在实现一些使妥协更加困难的目标：推翻伊朗神权政府、消除其核计划，以及摧毁伊朗盟友真主党在黎巴嫩的武装组织。周一，伊朗和以色列互相发动了袭击。

**美国袭击伊朗 伊朗反击海湾国家**

美国中央司令部表示，在伊朗日出前“完成”了最新一轮空袭。军方表示，这些袭击是“对伊朗无端且持续侵略的回应”，目标是“伊朗军事监视能力、通信系统和防空阵地”。对于袭击造成的具体损失，美方未做详细说明，但表示由美国空军、海军陆战队和海军执行了这些打击。

爆炸声在伊朗首都德黑兰、港口城市阿巴斯港以及其他霍尔木兹海峡沿线的南部地区回荡。

伊朗随即对巴林和科威特发动袭击，科威特关闭领空并抵御了攻击。科威特民航总局表示，航班正在改飞其他机场，但未提供更多细节。

科威特表示：“鉴于科威特遭受可耻的伊朗侵略，以及由此可能对地区民航交通造成的潜在风险，采取这一措施。”

科威特国际机场近日遭到伊朗直接袭击，造成一人死亡、数十人受伤。周三，伊朗在回应美国空袭时还对巴林、科威特和约旦发动了袭击，这些国家都驻有美军。

周四早些时候，以色列警告北部居民寻找避难所，此前探测到疑似来自黎巴嫩的来袭火力。美国驻约旦大使馆也发布警告，称“有报道显示导弹、无人机或火箭已进入约旦领空”。

约旦官方媒体尚未立即确认这些导弹袭击的报道。

**特朗普称美国秘密运输石油通过霍尔木兹海峡**

周三，国际基准原油价格交易超过每桶93美元，较战争开始以来上涨超过25%。

特朗普表示，美国军方自上月起执行了一项“秘密任务”，试图在霍尔木兹海峡突破伊朗的封锁。他说，在伊朗雷达设备被摧毁的帮助下，船只正在夜间秘密通过。

特朗普表示，结果超过1亿桶石油避开了伊朗对海峡的封锁。该数字未经证实，约为战争前该水道约五天的石油运输量。

军方角色尚不清楚。美国中央司令部发言人蒂姆·霍金斯上尉表示，美军在该地区“与商业船只进行沟通和协调”，但未详细说明提供的军事支持。

美国中央司令部周三反驳了伊朗关于霍尔木兹海峡已关闭的说法，称商业船只继续进出该水道。

**美国和伊朗的袭击震动中东**

周三早些时候，美国军方表示，一架美国飞机向帕劳旗船只“MT塞特贝洛”号发射“精确弹药”，当时该船试图突破封锁运送伊朗石油。这是美军在伊朗沿海水域击毁的第八艘商船。

印度外交部表示，塞特贝洛号被击中后，三名印度船员失踪，另有21人获救。声明未提及美军或封锁。

美国中央司令部的霍金斯表示，美军在开火前向船员发出警告。

美军表示，周三早些时候的袭击目标是“防空系统、地面控制站和 surveillance radar sites。”

Iran reported that U.S. strikes hit two water reservoirs in the southern city of Sirik, temporarily cutting off water to thousands of people. U.S. Central Command had no immediate comment. Tehran later claimed attacks in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi condemned the American attacks as a violation of Iranian sovereignty.

Still, efforts to mediate a deal continued. Following consultations with the U.S., a delegation from Qatar arrived in Tehran for talks earlier Wednesday, according to an official with knowledge of the visit who requested anonymity due to the sensitivity of the talks.

The exchanges of fire came a day after a U.S. Army attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz. The helicopter collided with an Iranian drone, according to a U.S. official speaking on condition of anonymity to discuss an ongoing investigation. It wasn't clear whether the collision was intentional.

A drone boat rescued the helicopter's two crew. Trump said they were uninjured.

**Big Disagreements Stand In The Way Of A Quick Peace Deal**

Wary of high gas prices in the run-up to congressional elections in November, Trump seems to be looking for a quick win. But he is also making demands that will be tough for Iran to swallow.

The U.S. wants to see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium. While Iran insists its nuclear program is peaceful, that uranium is a short technical step from weapons-grade levels.

Iran is refusing to give up the uranium and demanding relief from sanctions. It also wants the release of frozen assets even before a final agreement is in place, something Trump rejected.

Iran has insisted that any deal to end the war must also end fighting between its ally Hezbollah and Israel. Israel has instead intensified its military campaign against the Lebanon-based militant group.

Price reported from Washington. Associated Press writers Konstantin Toropin and Will Weissert in Washington; Edith M. Lederer at the United Nations; Natalie Melzer in Nahariya, Israel; David Rising in Bangkok; Bassem Mroue in Beirut; Michelle L. Price in New York; Russ Bynum in Savannah, Georgia; and Hannah Schoenbaum in Salt Lake City contributed to this report.
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United States launched a second round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations, and Iran responded with strikes targeting Bahrain and Kuwait.

The new U.S. assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted global energy supplies and sent oil prices higher.

Advertisement

It was the third time this week that back-and-forth strikes have tested a two-month ceasefire.

Trump has urged Iran to sign a deal to end the war and suggested earlier this week that an agreement could be reached in days.

Iran’s United Nations envoy said the U.S. should refrain from threats of force if it wants a deal.

“Iran has never negotiated under threats and pressure and will never submit to pressure or question,” Ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani told the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday.

Still, both countries seem to be looking for a way to end the conflict — if they can manage to sell it as a win at home.

Advertisement

Iran has proved resilient despite weeks of heavy bombing. It is betting that its ability to effectively close the Strait of Hormuz — a crucial passageway for oil and natural gas — gives it a strong bargaining chip.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appears intent on pursuing goals that make compromise harder: the collapse of Iran’s theocratic government, the elimination of its nuclear program, and the destruction of the Iranian-allied Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon. On Monday, Iran and Israel targeted each other.

Advertisement

U.S. Strikes Iran And Iran Fires Back At Gulf States

The U.S. Central Command said it had “completed” its latest round of airstrikes just before sunrise in Iran. The military command said the strikes came “in response to Iran’s unwarranted and continued aggression” and targeted “Iranian military surveillance capabilities, communication systems and air defense sites.” It did not elaborate on the damage done by the strikes, which it said were carried out by the U.S. Air Force, Marines and Navy.

Explosions from the strikes echoed around Iran’s capital, Tehran, as well as the port city of Bandar Abbas and other southern areas along the Strait of Hormuz.

Iran responded by launching strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait, and Kuwait closed its airspace as its air defenses fought off the attack. Kuwait’s Directorate General of Civil Aviation said flights were being diverted to other airports, without elaborating.

Advertisement

“This measure comes in light of the state of Kuwait being subjected to sinful Iranian aggressions and the potential risks that may result from this on civil aviation traffic in the region,” Kuwait said.

Kuwait International Airport took a direct Iranian hit in recent days, killing one person and wounding dozens. On Wednesday, Iran responded to U.S. strikes with attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, all of which host U.S. troops.

Israel early Thursday also warned residents in the north to seek shelter after the detection of suspected incoming fire from Lebanon, while the U.S. Embassy in Jordan issued a warning that “reports indicate missiles, drones or rockets are in Jordanian airspace.”

Advertisement

There was no immediate acknowledgment from Jordanian state media about the reports of missile fire.

Trump Says U.S. Is Sneaking Oil Past The Strait Of Hormuz

Since the U.S. and Israel started the war with Feb. 28 attacks on Iran, the conflict has shaken the global economy, driven up energy prices worldwide, and made food and other basics more expensive.

The international benchmark for crude oil traded above $93 a barrel on Wednesday, up more than 25% since the start of the war.

Advertisement

Trump said the U.S. military has since last month undertaken a “secret mission” to sneak oil shipments past Iran’s forces in the Strait of Hormuz. He said ships were slipping through at night, aided by the destruction of Iranian radar equipment.

Trump said as a result more than 100 million barrels of oil have evaded Iran’s chokehold on the strait. There was no immediate confirmation of that figure, which roughly equals five days of oil shipments through the waterway before the war began.

The military’s role was not immediately clear. Capt. Tim Hawkins, a Central Command spokesman, said U.S. forces “communicate and coordinate” with commercial ships in the area, but gave no details on military support being offered.

Advertisement

The U.S. Central Command on Wednesday refuted Iran’s claims that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, saying commercial ships are continuing to transit in and out.

U.S. And Iranian Strikes Shake The Mideast

Earlier Wednesday, the U.S. military said an American aircraft fired “precision munitions” into the engine room of the Palau-flagged vessel M/T Settebello as it attempted to breach the naval blockade with a shipment of Iranian oil. It was the eighth merchant vessel disabled by U.S. forces in waters off Iran.

India’s foreign ministry said three Indian sailors were missing after the Settebello was struck, while 21 others were rescued. Its statement did not mention the U.S. military or the blockade.

Advertisement

Hawkins of the U.S. Central Command said American forces warned the crew before firing on the ship.

The U.S. military said strikes earlier Wednesday targeted “air defense, ground control stations, and surveillance radar sites.”

Iran said U.S. strikes hit two water reservoirs in the southern city of Sirik, temporarily cutting off water to thousands of people. U.S. Central Command had no immediate comment. Tehran later claimed attacks in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi condemned the American attacks as a violation of Iranian sovereignty.

Advertisement

Still, efforts to mediate a deal continued. Following consultations with the U.S., a delegation from Qatar arrived in Tehran for talks earlier Wednesday, according to an official with knowledge of the visit who requested anonymity due to the sensitivity of the talks.

The exchanges of fire came a day after a U.S. Army attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz. The helicopter collided with an Iranian drone, according to a U.S. official speaking on condition of anonymity to discuss an ongoing investigation. It wasn’t clear whether the collision was intentional.

A drone boat rescued the helicopter’s two crew. Trump said they were uninjured.

Big Disagreements Stand In The Way Of A Quick Peace Deal

Wary of high gas prices in the run-up to congressional elections in November, Trump seems to be looking for a quick win. But he is also making demands that will be tough for Iran to swallow.

Advertisement

The U.S. wants to see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium. While Iran insists its nuclear program is peaceful, that uranium is a short technical step from weapons-grade levels.

Iran is refusing to give up the uranium and demanding relief from sanctions. It also wants the release of frozen assets even before a final agreement is in place, something Trump rejected.

Iran has insisted that any deal to end the war must also end fighting between its ally Hezbollah and Israel. Israel has instead intensified its military campaign against the Lebanon-based militant group.

Advertisement
